items.title: xUnit ledger-balance assertion test
items.description: For every posting document, write an xUnit test that submits the document and then asserts that the sum of debits equals the sum of credits in the resulting ledger rows, within rounding tolerance. This guards the core double-entry invariant against regressions in posting logic.
items.applicability: Generated accounting and inventory posting code under automated test.
items.example: [Fact] PostSalesInvoice_LedgerIsBalanced: post an invoice, query GL rows by voucher id, Assert.Equal(debits, credits) with a 0.005 delta.
items.limitation: A balanced ledger does not prove the accounts are correct; pair this with an assertion on the specific account ids and amounts to catch mis-mapped postings.

items.title: Negative-stock rejection test
items.description: Write a test that seeds a known on-hand quantity, attempts an outbound movement larger than the balance, and asserts that the operation throws/returns a domain error and that no stock-ledger row was written. Confirms the negative-stock guard is enforced.
items.applicability: Inventory issue, delivery, and transfer code in a generated ERP.
items.example: [Fact] Delivery_BeyondOnHand_IsRejected: seed 3 units, attempt delivery of 5, Assert.Throws<InsufficientStockException> and assert stock-ledger count unchanged.
items.limitation: Must also test the allow-negative-stock configuration path; a test that only covers the default forbids verifying the documented exception behaviour.

items.title: Maker-checker self-approval negative test
items.description: Write a negative test in which the same user who submitted a controlled document attempts to approve it, asserting the approval is rejected. Pair it with a positive test where a different authorised user approves successfully.
items.applicability: Approval workflow code that enforces segregation of duties.
items.example: [Fact] SelfApproval_IsRejected: user A submits payment, user A approves -> Assert.Throws<SelfApprovalException>. [Fact] DifferentApprover_Succeeds: user B approves -> status == Approved.
items.limitation: Negative tests must assert the specific failure reason, not just any exception, so an unrelated failure does not masquerade as the control working.

items.title: RBAC negative test for unauthorised action
items.description: For each controlled action, write a test that invokes it as a user lacking the required permission and asserts a 403/authorization failure with no side effect. This proves authorization is enforced server-side rather than only hidden in the UI.
items.applicability: Authorization layer of generated controllers and services.
items.example: [Fact] Accountant_CannotApproveJournal: act as Accountant role, call approve endpoint -> assert 403 and journal status still Pending.
items.limitation: Tests must exercise the API/service layer directly; asserting only that a button is hidden in a rendered view does not prove the endpoint is protected.

items.title: Posted-document immutability test
items.description: Submit a document, then attempt to edit or delete an accounting/stock-impacting field and assert the change is rejected. Confirms that only Draft documents are mutable and posted documents preserve the audit trail.
items.applicability: Document-lifecycle code for invoices, payments, journal entries, and stock entries.
items.example: [Fact] SubmittedInvoice_CannotBeEdited: submit invoice, attempt to change a line amount -> Assert.Throws<ImmutableDocumentException>; verify ledger rows unchanged.
items.limitation: Should also confirm permitted metadata edits still succeed, so the test does not over-lock the document beyond the documented immutability boundary.

items.title: Audit-trail record assertion test
items.description: After a controlled action, assert that exactly one audit row was appended with the expected actor, action, entity reference, and before/after values. Confirms the append-only audit trail captures the change.
items.applicability: Any state-changing operation that must be audited.
items.example: [Fact] Approve_WritesAuditRow: approve payment, query audit store -> assert one row with action=Approve, actor=B, prior=Pending, next=Approved.
items.limitation: The test should also assert no prior audit rows were mutated, since the value of the control is that history is append-only, not merely present.

items.title: Playwright login-flow test
items.description: Write a Playwright test that navigates to the login page, submits valid seeded credentials, and asserts the user lands on an authenticated landing page (e.g. a dashboard element is visible). This is the entry gate for all other E2E scenarios.
items.applicability: End-to-end validation of a generated ERP web UI.
items.example: await page.goto('/login'); fill username/password; click submit; await expect(page.getByTestId('dashboard-root')).toBeVisible().
items.limitation: Depends on deterministic seed credentials; a login test that relies on manually-created data is non-reproducible across fresh environments.

items.title: Page-renders and no-HTTP-500 smoke test
items.description: For each top-level page of a generated ERP, write a Playwright test that visits the route, asserts the response status is < 400, and asserts a known anchor element renders. Capture a screenshot on completion or failure as evidence.
items.applicability: Smoke validation that every generated page loads without server errors.
items.example: for each route in routes: const resp = await page.goto(route); expect(resp.status()).toBeLessThan(400); await expect(page.getByTestId(anchor)).toBeVisible(); await page.screenshot(...).
items.limitation: A page that renders without a 500 can still be functionally wrong; smoke tests prove availability, not correctness, and must be paired with behaviour assertions.

items.title: Use data-testid selectors for stable E2E
items.description: Generated UI must emit explicit data-testid attributes on interactive and assertion-target elements, and Playwright tests must select by those ids rather than by CSS classes or visible text. This keeps E2E tests stable when styling or copy changes.
items.applicability: Both the UI-generation templates and the E2E test generation in a generated ERP.
items.limitation: Selecting by localised visible text breaks under translation; relying on CSS classes breaks under restyle. The generator must consistently emit testids or the rule cannot be honoured.

items.title: Scenarios must assert business invariants, not just status codes
items.description: A scenario test is only meaningful if, after driving the flow, it asserts the domain invariant the scenario exists to protect (ledger balanced, stock conserved, control account reconciled, approval enforced). Asserting only that requests returned 200 produces green tests that hide broken business logic.
items.applicability: Design of every generated end-to-end and integration scenario test.
items.example: Quote-to-cash test does not stop at '200 on payment'; it asserts customer AR balance returned to zero and the GL is balanced after payment is applied.
items.limitation: Invariant assertions require querying the resulting data; scenarios written purely against the HTTP surface cannot inspect ledgers and must be paired with data-level checks.
